title: campanula scheuchzeri var. consanguinea
intro: Campanula scheuchzeri var. consanguinea, also known as Alpine bellflower, is a plant species belonging to the Campanulaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or blue flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Campanula scheuchzeri var. consanguinea include soothing coughs and colds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its uses and efficacy may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual needs.
